message:
fix(telemetry): Histogram::record fails closed on f64::INFINITY (ft-trot7)

Broaden the [ft-b4l62] NaN guard in Histogram::record to all non-finite
samples. Pre-fix, the guard caught NaN but missed ±INF, so a single
INF sample would:

  - poison total_sum permanently (finite + INF = INF, INF + INF = INF)
  - pin max to INF (or min to -INF) on the first non-finite sample
    because every later finite value compares as `finite > INF` = false
  - cause HistogramSummary to serialise the affected fields as JSON
    `null` (RFC 8259 forbids non-finite f64), silently dropping the
    value from downstream telemetry payloads

Reachable via legitimate division-by-zero in any client computing a
duration or rate as f64 (clock skew, mocked-time fixtures, denominator
that came in as 0.0). Same family as ft-b4l62 (NaN), ft-761tz
(disk_pressure NaN), ft-yskcu (continuous_backpressure NaN), ft-7s0vw
(event_id u64 overflow) — pathological numeric input cascades into a
silent wrong answer with no self-recovery.

Fix: `if !value.is_finite()` rejects NaN AND ±INF in one check.

Four regression tests pin the contract:
  - ft_trot7_histogram_record_pos_infinity_does_not_poison_mean
  - ft_trot7_histogram_record_neg_infinity_does_not_poison_mean
  - ft_trot7_histogram_all_infinity_keeps_mean_none
  - ft_trot7_histogram_summary_max_stays_finite_after_inf_attempt

Existing ft_b4l62_histogram_quantile_infinity_inputs_still_work
documents that INF as a *quantile* input (q parameter) is intentionally
clamped to [0,1] and still returns a real sample — that contract is
unchanged. The new guard sits on the *sample* (value) input path only.

All 74 histogram-matching tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
